income_thresholds.description: Annual family income must be below Rs 1,25,000 (Rs 1.25 lakh). BPL families are automatically eligible.
income_thresholds.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare / RAN Guidelines

exclusion_rules.description: Family annual income must be Rs 1,25,000 or below to qualify
Treatment must be at a government super-specialty hospital or institute (AIIMS, PGIMER, JIPMER, NIMHANS, etc.). Private hospitals are NOT eligible.
Only life-threatening diseases requiring super-specialty treatment are eligible. Routine or minor treatments do not qualify.
Application must be recommended by the Head of Department and Medical Superintendent of the treating government hospital

family_criteria.family_definition: Assistance is to the individual patient from a BPL family. Family income (all earning members combined) must be below Rs 1.25 lakh per annum.

covered_procedures: Heart surgery (open heart, valve replacement, bypass, congenital heart repair)
Cancer treatment (surgery, chemotherapy, radiation at government hospitals)
Any life-threatening disease requiring super-specialty intervention at government hospitals
excluded_procedures: Treatment already covered under PM-JAY or CGHS
Treatment for which other government scheme coverage is available

required_documents.name: BPL Certificate / Income Certificate
required_documents.local_name_hi: बीपीएल प्रमाण पत्र / आय प्रमाण पत्र
required_documents.notes: Income certificate from SDM/Tehsildar showing family income below Rs 1.25 lakh per annum

required_documents.name: Aadhaar Card
required_documents.notes: For identity verification and DBT payment

required_documents.name: Medical Report and Treatment Estimate
required_documents.notes: Detailed medical report with diagnosis, treatment plan, and cost estimate from the treating government hospital

required_documents.name: Recommendation from Medical Superintendent
required_documents.notes: Recommendation from HoD and Medical Superintendent of the government super-specialty hospital confirming need for financial assistance

required_documents.name: Bank Account Details
required_documents.notes: For fund transfer; account can be of patient or hospital (for direct transfer to hospital)

enrollment_channels: Government super-specialty hospital (AIIMS, PGIMER, JIPMER, etc.)
Medical Superintendent's office at treating hospital
Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (online/postal application)
enrollment_steps: Step 1: Get admitted at a government super-specialty hospital for life-threatening disease
Step 2: Treating doctor prepares medical report with diagnosis, treatment plan, and cost estimate
Step 3: Head of Department and Medical Superintendent recommend the case for RAN funding
Step 4: Patient/family submits application with BPL/income certificate, Aadhaar, and medical documents
Step 5: Hospital forwards application to Ministry of Health and Family Welfare or RAN Committee
Step 6: RAN Standing Committee reviews and approves the application
Step 7: Fund of up to Rs 15 lakh released directly to the hospital or patient's bank account
Step 8: Hospital provides treatment; any remaining amount returned to the fund
description_for_embedding: Rashtriya Arogya Nidhi (RAN) is a central government financial assistance scheme providing up to Rs 15 lakh for BPL patients with life-threatening diseases requiring super-specialty treatment at government hospitals (AIIMS, PGIMER, JIPMER, etc.). Family annual income must be below Rs 1.25 lakh. Only for life-threatening conditions like heart surgery, cancer, kidney transplant, and neurosurgery. Requires recommendation from the Medical Superintendent of the treating government hospital. Does not cover private hospital treatment. Application processed through the hospital and approved by the RAN Standing Committee at the Ministry of Health.
